headline: Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans
summary: Midjourney CEO David Holz unveiled The Midjourney Scanner, their first hardware product. It's an ultrasound-based full-body scanner, a significant shift from AI image generation. The company also plans to build a San Francisco spa for the new technology.

headline: Tim Cook says RAM expenses are 'unsustainable' and Apple is going to raise prices
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced that price increases are unavoidable due to rising RAM expenses. The company is facing huge increases in memory costs. Apple is trying to mitigate the impact but expects higher prices for consumers.

headline: VSCO launches Studio Pro mobile photo editing app and plans $500 per year subscription
summary: VSCO introduced Studio Pro, a new mobile photo editing app for iOS, with a macOS version planned. The app offers advanced features like batch editing and style matching, aiming to compete with Adobe. It will come with a steep $500 annual subscription.

headline: Epic wants to let you bring your Fortnite skins to other games
summary: Epic Games plans to allow Fortnite skins to be used across different games. This move is part of their broader vision for an interoperable metaverse using Unreal Engine 6. Developers will soon be able to build games that utilize Fortnite assets.

headline: Vampire Survivors Developer 'Reviewing' Fortnite Crossover Plans, After Epic Details Unreal Engine 6 AI Usage
summary: Vampire Survivors developer Poncle is reconsidering a potential Fortnite crossover. This decision follows Epic Games' announcement of using generative AI tools in Fortnite's art and design process. Concerns over AI implementation are impacting the lucrative partnership talks.

headline: Final Fantasy Tactics Remaster Adds New Game+, but War of the Lions Content Still Missing
summary: The Final Fantasy Tactics remaster has received an update, introducing a New Game+ mode. Fans are debating the necessity and potential of this new feature. Despite the update, content from the beloved War of the Lions version remains absent.

headline: Final Fantasy 14 Rewards Japanese Players for Paying Their Real-Life Taxes With a Giant Dog
summary: Final Fantasy 14 players in Japan can now earn in-game rewards by paying their real-life taxes. Rewards include various cosmetics and items. Notably, players can acquire the popular Megashiba Mount, a giant dog, for tax compliance.

headline: Apple Price Rises 'Unavoidable', Warns CEO Tim Cook
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook warned that price increases for Apple products are now 'unavoidable'. This is directly attributed to surging chip costs. The rising demand for AI technologies is largely to blame for these increased expenses.

headline: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
summary: A 14-paragraph memo outlines a new agreement between the US and Iran. Key terms include an end to active fighting and Iran's commitment to never possess nuclear weapons. Iran is also set to receive a $300 billion redevelopment package.

headline: Young women now have 'close to zero' risk of cervical cancer death after HPV jab
summary: A new study reveals that young women who received the HPV jab face 'close to zero' risk of cervical cancer death. The vaccine has saved hundreds of lives since its introduction to school-age girls in 2008. This highlights the profound impact of the HPV vaccination program.

headline: Church of England apologises for role in forced adoptions
summary: The Church of England has issued a formal apology for its involvement in forced adoptions. Between 1949 and 1976, thousands of unmarried women were pressured to give up their babies. The apology addresses decades of harm caused by these practices.

headline: Simpler, older version of Stonehenge found three miles from famous site
summary: Archaeologists have discovered a simpler, older version of Stonehenge. The site is located just three miles from the iconic landmark. This ancient structure comprised two posts that aligned perfectly with the solstices 5,000 years ago.
